title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.1
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
keywords: not, secrets, credentials, sec.1, hardcode, long-lived, secret-like, literals, should, live, secret, management
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.

title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.2
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
keywords: shell, not, command, sec.2, build, commands, trusted, primitives, concatenated, strings, execution, paths
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.

title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.3
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
keywords: text, sql, query, should, sec.3, parameterize, instead, composing, dynamically, built, variables, difficult
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: sql_injection, dynamic_sql
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.

title: Do not leave fallback secrets or test credentials in production code paths
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
summary: Backup credentials often become the least-audited credential in the system.
embedding_text:

language_id: shared
context_id:

keywords: fallback, secrets, credentials, sec.4, not, leave, test, production, code, paths, backup, often
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.

title: Keep command execution APIs structured so the executable and arguments remain distinct
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
summary: Security review is much stronger when command construction never depends on ad hoc shell parsing.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.

title: Dynamic query structure should stay allow-listed even when values are parameterized
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
summary: Parameter binding protects values, not uncontrolled structural SQL changes.

reviewability: auto_review

fix_guidance: Keep structural variation behind a small allow-list and continue binding values separately from the SQL text.
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
fix_guidance: Keep structural variation behind a small allow-list and continue binding values separately from the SQL text.

title: Example, bootstrap, and fallback credentials in committed code should be treated as real secrets unless clearly impossible to activate
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
summary: "Test" or "dev" labels do not remove rotation, misuse, or confusion risk once credentials are committed.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.

title: Avoid eval and other dynamic code execution paths
text: eval and similar APIs erase the normal static boundaries around code loading. They should be avoided in regular application logic.
summary: Runtime code generation is hard to secure and hard to reason about in review.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: eval_usage
fix_guidance: Replace dynamic execution with explicit dispatch tables, parsers, or data-driven logic.
text: eval and similar APIs erase the normal static boundaries around code loading. They should be avoided in regular application logic.
fix_guidance: Replace dynamic execution with explicit dispatch tables, parsers, or data-driven logic.

title: Treat innerHTML writes as an escaping boundary
text: Direct innerHTML assignment bypasses safer DOM construction patterns and creates a strong XSS boundary that reviewers must verify carefully.
summary: Rendering raw strings into the DOM should be exceptional and tightly controlled.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: innerhtml
fix_guidance: Prefer DOM APIs, templating with safe escaping, or a sanitized and narrowly bounded HTML rendering path.
text: Direct innerHTML assignment bypasses safer DOM construction patterns and creates a strong XSS boundary that reviewers must verify carefully.
fix_guidance: Prefer DOM APIs, templating with safe escaping, or a sanitized and narrowly bounded HTML rendering path.

title: Prefer text-safe DOM APIs over raw HTML sinks for normal rendering
text: Most UI updates only need text insertion or structured DOM creation. Using raw HTML assignment as the default rendering path widens the trust boundary unnecessarily.
summary: HTML injection surfaces should be explicit exceptions, not the default rendering primitive.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: innerhtml
fix_guidance: Use textContent, explicit element creation, or a rendering layer that escapes content by default.
text: Most UI updates only need text insertion or structured DOM creation. Using raw HTML assignment as the default rendering path widens the trust boundary unnecessarily.
fix_guidance: Use textContent, explicit element creation, or a rendering layer that escapes content by default.

title: Keep dynamic execution behind explicit dispatch or parsing boundaries
text: When behavior depends on external input or configurable actions, an explicit dispatch table or parser is easier to review, test, and secure than evaluating generated code.
summary: Application logic should not need eval-like execution to choose behavior at runtime.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: eval_usage
fix_guidance: Replace dynamic execution with named handlers, schemas, or parsers that keep the executable surface bounded.
text: When behavior depends on external input or configurable actions, an explicit dispatch table or parser is easier to review, test, and secure than evaluating generated code.
fix_guidance: Replace dynamic execution with named handlers, schemas, or parsers that keep the executable surface bounded.

title: document.write should be treated as a raw HTML injection boundary
text: Modern application code rarely needs document.write. When it appears, reviewers should assume a strong XSS and rendering-integrity boundary until proven otherwise.
summary: document.write mixes rendering, parsing, and DOM mutation in a way that is difficult to constrain safely.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: document_write
fix_guidance: Replace document.write with DOM construction, templating, or a safer rendering path that treats content as data by default.

fix_guidance: Replace document.write with DOM construction, templating, or a safer rendering path that treats content as data by default.

title: Prefer explicit async error propagation over promise chains that hide failures
text: Review asynchronous JavaScript for where errors go next. A chain that logs, swallows, or never returns the promise often creates reliability problems that are hard to observe.
summary: Promise chains are acceptable, but they should not obscure the failure path or detach it from callers.

language_id: javascript
context_id:

category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: promise_without_await
fix_guidance: Return or await the promise chain and keep one obvious error propagation path.
text: Review asynchronous JavaScript for where errors go next. A chain that logs, swallows, or never returns the promise often creates reliability problems that are hard to observe.
fix_guidance: Return or await the promise chain and keep one obvious error propagation path.

title: Prefer strict equality when coercion is not the point
text: The default comparison in maintainable code should avoid implicit coercion. If coercion is truly intended, the conversion should be explicit.
summary: Loose equality often hides surprising coercion rules and weakens intent clarity.

category: control_flow
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: loose_equality
fix_guidance: Use === or explicit normalization before comparing values from different sources.
text: The default comparison in maintainable code should avoid implicit coercion. If coercion is truly intended, the conversion should be explicit.
fix_guidance: Use === or explicit normalization before comparing values from different sources.

title: Return or await promise chains so failures stay attached to the caller
text: A promise chain that is not returned, awaited, or otherwise joined creates uncertainty about where errors land and whether later work is accidentally racing ahead.
summary: Detached asynchronous work is easy to miss in review and hard to observe in production.

category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: promise_without_await
fix_guidance: Await the promise or return the chain so the next layer owns the success and failure contract.
text: A promise chain that is not returned, awaited, or otherwise joined creates uncertainty about where errors land and whether later work is accidentally racing ahead.
fix_guidance: Await the promise or return the chain so the next layer owns the success and failure contract.

title: Normalize values explicitly before comparison instead of relying on implicit coercion
text: Values that arrive from requests, storage, or the DOM often deserve parsing or normalization before comparison so the code communicates which representation is authoritative.
summary: Type coercion is a hidden control-flow branch that reviewers should not have to simulate mentally.

title: Next.js route handlers should validate request.json() payloads before trusting shape
text: Route handlers are transport boundaries. Pulling request.json() directly into business logic widens the trust boundary unless validation is immediately reintroduced.
summary: Raw JSON parsing bypasses the schema step that makes route contracts reviewable.

fix_guidance: Parse into a narrow boundary object and validate the payload immediately before the rest of the route uses it.

title: Server actions should validate FormData values before domain use
text: Server actions still accept user-controlled transport data. Pulling raw FormData values into domain logic without validation makes trust boundaries harder to audit.
summary: FormData is transport input, not proof of a typed server-side contract.

fix_guidance: Convert FormData into a validated boundary object before invoking domain or persistence logic.
text: Server actions still accept user-controlled transport data. Pulling raw FormData values into domain logic without validation makes trust boundaries harder to audit.

title: Client components must not depend on non-public server environment variables
text: A use client component runs in a browser-facing environment. Reading non-public env vars there either leaks intent, breaks at runtime, or obscures where the real secret boundary is supposed to live.
summary: The client boundary should not be the place where server-only secrets quietly leak or fail open.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: next_client_secret_env
fix_guidance: Keep secrets on the server boundary and pass only explicitly public, derived values into client components.
text: A use client component runs in a browser-facing environment. Reading non-public env vars there either leaks intent, breaks at runtime, or obscures where the real secret boundary is supposed to live.
fix_guidance: Keep secrets on the server boundary and pass only explicitly public, derived values into client components.

title: Browser APIs in app-router components require an explicit client boundary
text: App-router components default to server rendering semantics. Reaching for browser globals without an explicit client boundary is a strong signal that the rendering contract may be drifting.
summary: Using window or document without a visible use client contract makes the server-client split hard to trust.
